questions.question: Which is the first Indian bank to get Reserve Bank of India (RBI) approval for rupee trade?
questions.explanation: India’s Public sector lender UCO Bank is the first bank to receive the Reserve Bank of India’s (RBI’s) approval for rupee trade. The Kolkata-based bank will open a special vostro account with Gazprombank of Russia for trade settlement in Indian rupees. In July, RBI announced its decision to allow Indian banks to settle trade in Indian currency in July. RBI also allowed trade settlements between India and other countries, including Sri Lanka and Russia.

questions.question: Which personality’s birth anniversary is observed as ‘Antyodaya Diwas’ in India?
questions.explanation: To commemorate the birth anniversary Pandit Deendayal Upadhyaya , September 25 is celebrated as Antyodaya Diwas in India since 2014. Deendayal Upadhyaya mobilised the concept of ‘Antyodaya’ which literally means, “the rise of the last person.” He stressed on Antyodaya to target the extreme poverty existed in India during and after the independence.

questions.question: Duleep Trophy, which was held recently, is associated with which sports?
questions.explanation: The Duleep Trophy final between the West Zone and South Zone was recently held in the city of Coimbatore, Tamil Nadu. It is a domestic first-class cricket competition played in India. In this year’s edition, West Zone defeated South Zone by 294 runs, and won the title. Ajinkya Rahane was the skipper of the West Zone cricket team.

questions.question: Operation ‘Megha Chakra’, which was seen in the news, is associated with which institution?
questions.explanation: As part of Operation ‘Megha Chakra’, the Central Bureau of Investigation (CBI) carried out searches at 56 locations in 19 states and a UT in connection with two cases of circulation of Child Sexual Abuse Material (CSAM) online. The recent searches were based on inputs from Interpol Singapore and intelligence obtained during Operation Carbon, conducted last year against peddlers of CSAM on Internet.

questions.question: Which Indian PSU has signed MoU with Brazilian oil major Petrobras, to diversify its crude oil sourcing?
questions.explanation: Indian major Public sector Unit Bharat Petroleum Corporation Ltd (BPCL) has signed a Memorandum of Understanding (MoU) with Brazilian oil major Petrobras, to diversify its crude oil sourcing. Bharat PetroResources Limited (BPRL) plans to invest USD 1.6 billion to develop an oil block in Brazil to procure equity oil overseas. For the first time, India will ink a long-term supply deal with Oil major- Petrobras that operates in the oil, natural gas and energy industry.

questions.question: Which city is the host of the ‘Global Skills Summit’ 2022?
questions.explanation: The 13th Global Skills Summit 2022 has been organized by FICCI in New Delhi. The theme of the Summit is Education to Employability – Making it Happen. Union Minister for Education, Skill Development & Entrepreneurship, Dharmendra Pradan inaugurated and addressed the Summit. Representatives from World Bank, ILO, UNDP, UNICEF and Industry leaders from organizations like LinkedIn, Amazon, Adobe will participate in the summit.

questions.question: What is PMGKAY, which was recently extended till December 2022?
questions.explanation: The Central Government has decided to extend the free food-grain scheme for those below the poverty line (BPL), for another three months till December 31, 2022. This will be the seventh phase of the Pradhan Mantri Garib Kalyan Anna Yojana (PMGKAY) which will cost the Centre an additional Rs 44,762 crore.

questions.question: Which state inaugurated the ‘Lata Mangeshkar Chowk’ at the banks of the Saryu river?
questions.explanation: Uttar Pradesh Chief Minister Yogi Adityanath inaugurated the Lata Mangeshkar Chowk in Ayodhya on the occasion of the 93rd birth anniversary of the late singer. The intersection at the banks of the Saryu river has been developed at an estimated cost of ₹7.9 crore. The legendry singer and Bharat Ratna awardee died February 2022 at the age of 92.

questions.question: ‘Operation Garuda’, which was seen in the news, is associated with which organisation?
questions.explanation: The Central Bureau of Investigation has launched a multi-phase ‘Operation Garuda’ against illicit drug trafficking network. This global operation was initiated in coordination with Interpol and Narcotics Control Bureau, to combat smuggling of illicit drugs and psychotropic substances, with focus on Indian Ocean region. CBI registered 127 new cases, arrested 175 people and seized huge quantities of narcotic drugs during this special operation.

questions.question: Which organisation released ‘Guidelines on mental health at work’?
questions.explanation: The World Health Organization (WHO) and International Labour Organization (ILO) have issued guidelines to address mental health issues among the global workforce. The guidelines recommended measures to address threats to mental health such as excessive workloads and negative behaviours. As per WHO, Depression and anxiety account for 12 billion lost workdays annually, costing the world economy close to USD 1 trillion.
